architecture_note: Approved content only. Runtime AI generation is not allowed. Search may retrieve approved cards only.
content_rules: Use universal language in displayed fields so scripts work across family structures and situations.
Do not use relationship-specific words in displayed fields such as sister, brother, mom, dad, baby, friend, classmate, or teacher.
Hidden search terms may include words parents might type, but displayed scripts must stay broadly applicable.
The app must never generate new parenting scripts, actions, helpful notes, or safety notes at runtime.
The Show another phrase button must rotate only through approved cards with the same category_id, behavior_id, age_band_id, and context_id.
Food content must avoid shame, dieting language, weight comments, moralizing, and forced eating language.
Food-security behaviors should pair reassurance with a practical boundary.
This content is a quick draft for tough moments, not therapy, diagnosis, medical advice, nutrition counseling, or eating-disorder treatment.

contexts.label: Calm this down
contexts.description: Use when food behavior is emotional, anxious, resistant, or escalating but there is not active danger.

contexts.label: Stop unsafe behavior
contexts.description: Use when food behavior is unsafe, unsanitary, physically aggressive, choking-related, secretive at night, or likely to cause harm.

contexts.label: Repair afterward
contexts.description: Use after the immediate moment has passed to reduce shame, clean up, reconnect, and make a predictable plan.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food is safe. Food stays where it can stay clean. I will help.
cards.do_this: Use a calm voice and show the next food option or snack place. Do not interrogate or grab food from the child's hands unless it is unsafe.
cards.helpful_note: Food hoarding often comes from fear, not defiance. Reassurance plus a clean-food boundary works better than shame.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: You wanted to keep food close. Food is not for the room. We will make a safe place.
cards.do_this: Walk with the child to put the food in a kitchen spot, then offer a small predictable snack if appropriate.
cards.helpful_note: The child needs to hear that food is available while learning where food belongs.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I see the food. You are not in trouble. Food needs a clean place.
cards.do_this: Stay neutral. Remove spoiled or open food calmly and replace fear with a simple plan.
cards.helpful_note: Neutral language lowers panic and helps the child trust that food will not disappear.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Stop. This food is not safe now. I am moving it.
cards.do_this: Remove spoiled, opened, or choking-risk food. Keep your voice flat and offer water or a safe snack later if appropriate.
cards.helpful_note: Unsafe food needs action without panic or shame.
cards.safety_note: If the child may have eaten spoiled food or is choking, vomiting, or struggling to breathe, seek urgent help.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I will keep your body safe. This food goes away now.
cards.do_this: Take unsafe food out of reach and clean the area calmly.
cards.helpful_note: The child can feel the adult is protective instead of punitive.
cards.safety_note: For choking, breathing trouble, or repeated vomiting, use emergency or medical support.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food has to be clean to eat. I am helping keep it safe.
cards.do_this: Move the child away from unsafe food and redirect to handwashing or water.
cards.helpful_note: Short safety words work best for very young children.
cards.safety_note: Contact a medical professional if there are signs of illness after eating hidden food.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food is safe. Food has a clean place. We will try again.
cards.do_this: Show the child the clean food place and practice putting one item there.
cards.helpful_note: Tiny practice builds the rule without shaming the fear.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: You wanted food close. Next time, we ask for help.
cards.do_this: Practice the words 'food please' or point to the snack spot.
cards.helpful_note: A replacement request gives the child a safer way to get reassurance.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: We cleaned it up. You are safe. Food comes again.
cards.do_this: End with connection and a predictable next food cue.
cards.helpful_note: Reassurance after cleanup helps reduce future hiding.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: You do not have to hide food to be safe. Food stays where it can stay clean.
cards.do_this: Help move the food without lecturing. Show the child where a safe snack or next meal can be found.
cards.helpful_note: This gives reassurance and a boundary at the same time.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Your body may be worried there will not be enough. I will help you know when food is coming.
cards.do_this: Point to the next snack or meal plan. Keep the conversation short and practical.
cards.helpful_note: Predictability reduces the need to stash food.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food is safe here. We are going to move this back and make a plan.
cards.do_this: Use a basket, shelf, or written snack plan if available. Avoid making the child confess why it happened.
cards.helpful_note: A plan teaches more than a shame-based consequence.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: This food is not safe to eat. I am moving it now. You are not in trouble.
cards.do_this: Remove unsafe food first, then wash hands and clean the storage area.
cards.helpful_note: Saying 'not in trouble' helps prevent the child from hiding more next time.
cards.safety_note: If the child ate spoiled food or has repeated vomiting, call a medical professional.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food in the room can get dirty or spoiled. We are cleaning it up now.
cards.do_this: Use one clear task: put wrappers here, food there, hands washed after.
cards.helpful_note: A simple cleanup keeps the focus on safety, not shame.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I will not let you eat food that might hurt your body. We will find safe food after this is cleaned up.
cards.do_this: Remove unsafe items and offer a predictable next food option when appropriate.
cards.helpful_note: The child needs both protection and reassurance that food still exists.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: You were worried about food. Hiding it made a mess. Let us make a safer plan.
cards.do_this: Have the child help with a small cleanup, then show the next snack or meal plan.
cards.helpful_note: Repair should teach safety without making the child feel ashamed for food fear.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food can feel scary when you are not sure what comes next. We will make it clear.
cards.do_this: Create a simple visual: next meal, next snack, and where food belongs.
cards.helpful_note: Predictability is the repair.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Next time, ask for the food plan instead of hiding food.
cards.do_this: Practice the sentence once and choose the clean food place together.
cards.helpful_note: Practicing the exact words makes the next moment easier.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I am not angry that you wanted food. Hidden food can get unsafe, so we need a better plan.
cards.do_this: Ask the child to help move the food to a clean place, then agree on where approved snacks will be kept.
cards.helpful_note: This protects dignity while addressing the sanitation issue.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: You do not need to sneak food to make sure you have enough. Let us make the food plan visible.
cards.do_this: Show the next food time or create a simple snack agreement. Keep the focus on safety and trust.
cards.helpful_note: A visible plan can calm food-scarcity fear.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food in the room is not safe long term. Food anxiety is real, and we can handle it another way.
cards.do_this: Remove hidden food calmly and choose one safe storage option together.
cards.helpful_note: Separating the fear from the rule keeps the child from feeling labeled as bad.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: This has become a food-safety issue. We are cleaning it up now, and then we will make a plan.
cards.do_this: Remove spoiled food, wrappers, and anything that could attract pests. Keep tone businesslike.
cards.helpful_note: Businesslike cleanup lowers humiliation and gets the environment safe.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I cannot leave hidden food here if it could make anyone sick. Help me clear it, then we will talk.
cards.do_this: Start cleanup before discussion. Use gloves or bags if needed and avoid lecturing while cleaning.
cards.helpful_note: Safety comes first; explanation works better after the urgent part is handled.
cards.safety_note: If there are signs of illness, repeated vomiting, or choking risk, contact medical support.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Food anxiety is real. Unsafe food still has to go.
cards.do_this: Validate once, then remove the unsafe food and reset the storage plan.
cards.helpful_note: This keeps compassion and limits together.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Let us repair the mess and make a plan that helps food feel more predictable.
cards.do_this: Ask for cleanup help, then agree on a safe snack option or food reassurance routine.
cards.helpful_note: Repair combines responsibility with reassurance.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: You are not bad for wanting food. Hidden food still cannot stay in the room.
cards.do_this: Talk through what triggered the hiding and what the child can do next time.
cards.helpful_note: Naming the trigger prevents the repair from becoming only a consequence.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: We need trust and food safety. Let us set up both.
cards.do_this: Create a written plan for snacks, leftovers, or asking for food outside meal times.
cards.helpful_note: A concrete plan gives control without secrecy.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I am not here to shame you for wanting food. Hidden food can become unsafe, so we need a cleaner plan.
cards.do_this: Offer privacy and a direct plan: where food can be kept, when food is available, and what to do when anxiety spikes.
cards.helpful_note: Teens need dignity. The boundary is about sanitation and safety, not character.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: You do not have to prove why you kept it. We do need food to stay where it is safe and clean.
cards.do_this: Avoid a courtroom conversation. Move straight to a practical storage and snack agreement.
cards.helpful_note: This reduces defensiveness and keeps the relationship intact.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: If food feels uncertain, tell me that directly. Hiding it in the room is not the plan we can use.
cards.do_this: Collaborate on a discreet safe snack plan that does not involve hidden food.
cards.helpful_note: A teen is more likely to use a plan that protects privacy and trust.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I am not going to shame you, but I cannot leave food here if it is unsafe or attracting pests.
cards.do_this: Ask the teen to help clear the food privately. Keep the tone respectful and matter-of-fact.
cards.helpful_note: Privacy helps a teen stay engaged instead of defensive.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: This is a health and sanitation issue now. We are handling that first.
cards.do_this: Remove unsafe food, clean the area, and save the deeper conversation for after the room is safe.
cards.helpful_note: The immediate task is safety, not a lecture about responsibility.
cards.safety_note: Seek medical help if there is concern about spoiled food, repeated vomiting, or choking.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: We can talk about the food worry after the unsafe food is gone.
cards.do_this: Create distance from the unsafe food and use a calm cleanup plan.
cards.helpful_note: Separating cleanup from emotional processing prevents escalation.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: We need to repair the sanitation issue and make a food plan that does not rely on hiding.
cards.do_this: Let the teen help set the plan: approved food location, privacy, timing, and what to do when food anxiety hits.
cards.helpful_note: Respectful collaboration supports buy-in.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: I care more about solving this than blaming you. What food worry are we trying to plan for?
cards.do_this: Ask one calm question, then build one practical adjustment.
cards.helpful_note: The goal is safer behavior, not a confession.

cards.category_subtitle: Calm the table without power struggles
cards.say_this: Next time, use the plan before food ends up hidden in the room.
cards.do_this: Write or save the plan somewhere private and easy to access.
cards.helpful_note: A private plan protects dignity while keeping food safe.
